title: Uthman 's Exile of Certain Bagrans to Syria
description: It was transmitted to me in writing by al-Sari --Shu'ayb-Sayf'Atiyyah-Yazid al-Faq'asi: When Ibn 'Amir had been governor for three years, he learned that there was a man in [the residential quarter of the tribe of] 'Abd al-Qays who was staying with 223. The name in brackets is supplied by Magrizi, al-Mugaffa; see de Goeje, Introductio, dcxxvii. With the names supplied here and in note zig, the list totals ten-that is, the number of troublemakers mentioned in Sa'id's first letter to 'Uthman above, p.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 15
